name: nepeta cataria
intensity: Mild in typical tea-level use; stronger effects are uncommon.
mechanism: Volatile terpenoids may contribute smooth-muscle, antimicrobial, or sensory effects.
safetyNotes: May cause moderate risk: side effects or medication interactions are plausible. avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, alcohol, or other cns depressants or drowsiness. Avoid combining with sedatives; Avoid in pregnancy due to uterine stimulation; Avoid in pregnancy; Avoid with sedation-sensitive individuals.

sources.title: PubMed search: Nepeta cataria
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Nepeta cataria is a medicinal herb traditionally used for mild sedation and relaxation.
sideEffects: avoid in pregnancy due to uterine stimulation
